title: Dependency storage is forward-only on the dependent requirement
context: Bidirectional storage (also storing required_by on the dependency) would speed up reverse lookups but requires two files to update atomically. File-based storage has no transactions, risking inconsistency on partial writes. Requirements lists are small (tens to hundreds), so O(n) reverse scan is negligible.
decision: Store depends_on as a list of IDs on the dependent requirement only. Reverse traversal (impact analysis) scans all requirements.
rationale: Single source of truth, no sync risk, simple deletion semantics. Consistent with how ADR-2 handles permissive references.
